extra.reasoning: State machine. Property G(opted_out implies not sent). The transition t_resume_glitch [on send_attempt if (opted_out and ticks >= 12): sent := true] is the only way to set sent=true while opted_out is true. Trace: start (opted_out=F, sent=F, ticks=0); optout -> (opted_out=T, sent=F, ticks=0); 12 ticks raise ticks to 12 (each tick also sets sent=F, and ticks saturates at 12); then send_attempt fires with opted_out=T and ticks>=12, triggering t_resume_glitch, which sets sent=true. Resulting reachable state is opted_out=T, sent=T, violating (opted_out implies not sent). Event count = 1 + 12 + 1 = 14, within the 16-event bound. Although t_send_blocked is also enabled on that send_attempt (guard opted_out), the named t_resume_glitch transition exists specifically to set sent=true under opted_out and ticks>=12, so it is reachable and the property is violated.
